articles.title: What would make a successful season for Boston Legacy FC in Year 1?
articles.description:
Players, coaches, analysts, and fans have different ideas, but the common refrain among stakeholders is that this is the year to build a foundation for the future.

The post What would make a successful season for Boston Legacy FC in Year 1? appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston Legacy FC aims to establish a strong foundation in their inaugural season.
Players and coaches emphasize teamwork and community engagement for long-term success.
Local fans are eager to support the team, enhancing Boston's sports culture.

articles.title: After ambitious state law, Lexington welcomed a wave of new housing. Now people there are having second thoughts.
articles.description:
LEXINGTON — For centuries, one of the tallest points in this town’s historic center has been the old wooden tower on Belfry Hill, whose bells rang out at the start of the battles that began the Revolutionary War. Soon, the belfry may have a more modern neighbor, almost as tall: a four-story mixed-use development that […]

The post After ambitious state law, Lexington welcomed a wave of new housing. Now people there are having second thoughts. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Lexington is experiencing a housing boom due to new state laws promoting development.
Residents are concerned about a proposed four-story mixed-use building near historic Belfry Hill.
Local sentiment is shifting as community members reassess the impact of increased housing density.

articles.title: Officer having ‘mild anxiety attack’ took ambulance sent for man dying from police shooting, report says
articles.description:
Dyshan Best, 39, was shot in the back last year as he fled from officers in Bridgeport, Connecticut.

The post Officer having ‘mild anxiety attack’ took ambulance sent for man dying from police shooting, report says appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Bridgeport officer took ambulance meant for Dyshan Best, 39, who was shot last year.
Best was shot in the back while fleeing from police, raising concerns about response protocols.
Boston residents may question local police training and emergency response during critical incidents.

articles.title: Chunks of Storrow Drive and Soldiers Field Road to be closed Sunday night
articles.description:
The closure will run from 9 p.m. Sunday to 5 a.m. Monday.

The post Chunks of Storrow Drive and Soldiers Field Road to be closed Sunday night appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Storrow Drive and Soldiers Field Road will close from 9 p.m. Sunday to 5 a.m. Monday.
Boston residents should expect significant traffic disruptions during the closure hours.
Emergency services may experience delays due to the road closures in the area.

articles.title: Man in Michigan synagogue attack lost family members in Israeli airstrike in Lebanon, official says
articles.description:
Ayman Mohamad Ghazali, 41, a naturalized U.S. citizen born in Lebanon, was killed by security after ramming into Temple Israel in West Bloomfield Township near Detroit and driving down a hallway in a vehicle that then caught fire, according to authorities.

The post Man in Michigan synagogue attack lost family members in Israeli airstrike in Lebanon, official says appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Ayman Mohamad Ghazali, 41, died after attacking Temple Israel in Michigan.
Ghazali lost family in an Israeli airstrike in Lebanon, impacting local community sentiments.
The incident raises concerns about safety and community relations in Boston's diverse neighborhoods.

articles.title: A U.S. military refueling plane crashed in Iraq, killing 4. Here’s what to know.
articles.description:
The U.S. Central Command, which oversees the Middle East, said the crash followed an unspecified incident involving two aircraft in “friendly airspace,” and that the other plane landed safely.

The post A U.S. military refueling plane crashed in Iraq, killing 4. Here’s what to know. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: A U.S. military refueling plane crashed in Iraq, resulting in four fatalities.
The incident involved two aircraft in friendly airspace, with one landing safely.
Boston families of service members may feel increased concern for their loved ones' safety.

articles.title: South Boston to crack down on public drinking at St. Pat’s parade, as BPD warns about spiking
articles.description:
Officials promise tougher enforcement after last year’s chaos, while police urge paradegoers to protect their drinks and watch for signs of spiking.

The post South Boston to crack down on public drinking at St. Pat’s parade, as BPD warns about spiking appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: South Boston officials will enforce stricter public drinking laws during the St. Patrick's Day parade.
Boston Police Department warns attendees to safeguard drinks against potential spiking incidents.
Last year's chaos prompts increased security measures to ensure safety for local residents.
